rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WESTERLIND VS. ALLIED PACKING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 340.2(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 6, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANTS MAREMONT CORPORATION, KELSEY-HAYES COMPANY AND ARVINMERITOR, INC'S MOTION To Dismiss For Failure To Commence Wrongful Death Action Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 340.2(C) is granted. The decision in Barr v. A C and S, Inc. 57 Cal. App. 4th 1038, establishes that the 1 year statute of limitations of CCP section 340.2(c)(1) applies and given decedent's death in October 2006, this action now must be dismissed. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MOIRA KERANS VS. RANDALL B. WEIL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 6, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT RANDALL WEIL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence. Plaintiff presents evidence that creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff had the right to rely on Dr. Weil's assurances (see Unjian v. Berman (1989) 208 Cal. App. 3d 881) and her declaration is not subject to a motion to strike under D'Amico. As a consequence the claim is not barred by CCP 340.5. Dr. Hviestendahl's declaration is admissible to create a triable issue of fact. The declaration states what the witness relied upon and since he performed surgery on plaintiff, Dr. Hviestendahl was in position to understand the problems with the prior surgery. Although the opinion set forth is not expressed in standard phrases, the import is sufficient to defeat a motion for summary judgment where all inferences are to be given to plaintiff's evidence. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LEXIS NEXIS VS. TIM GOMES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 6, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT TIM GOMES' MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant was served. As an attorney, he understood the need to respond. His reasons for not doing so do not constitute excusable neglect and defendant has failed to show why he delayed in seeking this relief. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID REPLOGLE VS. RICHARD ZITRIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Special Mtn To Strike The Complaint As A Meritless Slapp
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 6, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANTS RICHARD ZITRIN, PAUL FRASETTO AND ZITRIN & FRASETTO, LLP'S Special Motion To Strike The Complaint As A Meritless SLAPP is granted. Defendants have demonstrated that the conduct at issue is protected activity under the first prong of CCP 425.16 (e)(1) and (2) as a written statement made in a judicial proceeding in a matter under consideration. Plaintiff has failed to present admissible evidence that establishes a probability of prevailing. The conduct at issue is protected by the litigation privilege, Civil Code 47(b). Silberg v. Anderson (1990) 50 Cal. 3d 205, 212. =(302/PJM/VC)
